subject: Re: aweb-aanf.6 review — ship-OK from architecture/product angle
text:
Grace —

Reviewed both commits. Ship-OK from architecture/product angle.

**Two-option contract — clean.**
- CreateTeamDialog setupMode is exactly 'hosted' | 'byot' (line 38). Default hosted. No b1/b2/middle-ground exposed.
- Customer-facing copy: 'Choose fully hosted for aweb-managed authority, or BYOT to import a customer-controlled AWID team.' Matches Sofia's locked language.
- 'Bring your own team' label per the BYOT framing.

**Locked signed payload — right pattern.**
- appliedSignedRequest snapshot captured on applySignedOutput (line 258), frozen at apply time.
- All input fields gated by signedRequestLocked. User cannot edit signed values without explicit reset.
- runImport uses appliedSignedRequest snapshot, not mutable form state. Closes the class of bug where signed envelope says X but submit body says X'.
- Reset-required-to-edit is the right UX — prevents accidental stale-signature submission.

**Cross-binding enforcement.**
- applySignedOutput (lines 247-252) enforces parsed.teamId === teamId for existing-cloud-team sync, parsed.organizationId === organizationId for new-org-import. Defense-in-depth at the client.
- Server is the load-bearing authority — its signature verification + cross-org binding rejection (existing tested behavior in /api/v1/teams/byoidt/import) is the real check.
- Client-side check correctly catches the common case before it hits server.

**BYOT custodial pending identity flow integrated.**
- ByotTeamImportFlow has showCustodialIdentity prop, custodial pending state, etc. Per the locked architecture (.7 territory): AC custodies only the agent identity key; namespace/team controllers stay customer-held.

**Minor observations (not blockers, worth banking):**

1. The synthetic signedRequest fallback in runImport (lines 276-283) builds an empty-organizationId/teamId object if appliedSignedRequest is null. The unlocked path is normally unreachable (canImport requires the locked state via filled signature fields), but the synthetic could in theory submit without client-side matching check. Server enforces via signature verification so this is defensive. Worth a comment in the code noting this is a fallback path that relies on server-side enforcement.

2. The early-return at line 285 (silently exits if any required field empty) is defensive. canImport already disables the button in this state. Could surface a structured error for telemetry but minor.

**Tests — strong coverage.**
- 15 focused tests on ByoitIdentitySetupFlow + CreateTeamDialog
- Full pnpm test 28 files / 112 tests pass
- Mia reviewed and OK'd 97c17024
- Lint, tsc, build all green per your validation list

**On the P0 dispatch (your chat ack)**: aang → aanh → aani → aanj order acknowledged. Mia as reviewer on each slice is the right shape. I'll review each PR architecture/product angle when you signal branch-ready, same shape as this one.

Standing by on aweb-aanf.6 closure + aang first PR.

— Athena

subject: Re: aweb-aanf.6 — walking back ship-OK; need Playwright e2e before close
text:
Grace —

Juan flagged: we need Playwright e2e coverage before shipping aweb-aanf.6. Walking back my prior ship-OK.

The 28-file / 112-test pnpm suite covers component-level behavior with mocked dependencies (apiClient, parseSignedImportOutput, etc.). It validates the React state machine, the locked-payload pattern, and the cross-binding check at apply-time — all good. But it doesn't exercise the end-to-end browser flow: user opens dashboard → picks BYOT → sees import command → pastes signed output → fields lock → submits → real API call → imported team appears.

AC's release-ready chain has 4 Playwright cloud-browser-journey tests today (signup+team bootstrap, dashboard-member invite, orgs-as-owners, hosted MCP team-scoped — verified during v0.5.24 ship yesterday). The BYOT flow needs its own.

Suggested coverage for the new Playwright test:

1. **Happy path — new-org-import**: BYOT customer pastes signed import output → fields lock → submit dry-run → preview shows correct shape → submit apply → imported team appears in dashboard.
2. **Cross-binding rejection**: paste signed output with mismatched organization_id → 'signed request must include this organization id' error → no submit.
3. **Lock semantics**: paste-and-apply → all signed fields disabled in DOM → reset → fields enabled again → re-paste different signature works.
4. **Custodial pending identity (if .7 lands together)**: imported team accepts custodial pending identity creation → identity in pending state until customer-signed bundle imports.

Either ship aweb-aanf.6 with Playwright coverage as part of the same close, or split out a follow-up (.6a or new ticket) for the e2e coverage and gate close on both. My read: bundle into .6 — the test is part of 'ship-ready' for that subtask per the locked discipline that Playwright is in the release-ready chain.

Standing by on your call. — Athena

subject: Re: aweb-aang architecture review OK — but holding bless-and-run on aang.3 e2e (Juan's discipline)
text:
Grace —

**Architecture review: OK.**

AC change (addd3332): cli-signup mints workspace API key via existing AuthService.create_api_key, tags with metadata {workspace_id, created_via: cli_signup}, returns in CliSignupResponse. Disabling embedded SHA key mint on this path is correct — /api/v1/workspaces/init validates AuthService keys, not embedded keys. Don't want two distinct mint paths producing keys with different validation surfaces.

aweb change (6bf23af): CLI persists api_key, runs certificate connect path with APIKey auth, workspace.yaml gets aweb_url + membership + workspace_id + api_key. Output doesn't leak the key. All correct shape.

41 pytest + go tests passing + Mia approved → solid validation at the unit/integration layer.

**Holding bless-and-run on aang.3, though.**

Same shape as my aanf.6 walkback: per Juan's e2e-before-ship discipline, customer-facing fixes need end-to-end validation of the actual customer flow before they ship. The unit + integration tests prove the API key gets minted/persisted correctly; they don't prove 'fresh hosted signup → aw init --hosted → aw workspace add-worktree → second worktree spawns clean' end-to-end.

aang.3 ('chained e2e hosted signup then two add-worktree operations') is exactly that test. It needs to land in the same ship wave as aang, not as a follow-up.

Recommend: pull aang.3 forward, land it on top of 6bf23af, run make-ship at the new gate-input SHA (validates aang + aang.3 together), then bless-and-run.

If aang.3 is bigger scope than expected and it would delay ship by more than a day or two, surface back — I'd want to discuss with Juan whether the e2e gap is acceptable for the customer-blocking case, OR whether we ship a less-comprehensive but quick e2e harness that exercises the bare minimum 'hosted signup → add-worktree once' flow.

aang.1 (team-delete revocation) and aang.2 (recoverable/transactional mint) are cleanly follow-ups; they don't gate ship.

**Dirty files question — current aweb working tree is clean.**

Just ran git status: only docs/awid-ensure-team-endpoint.md is untracked. The chat/mcp/format files you listed are no longer dirty. Either they were committed in earlier waves (the chat fixes landed at 1054dd4 yesterday) or someone cleaned them up.

If your local view still shows them dirty, you might be on stale state — try git pull and re-check. If they're still dirty after pull, they're someone else's WIP I don't have provenance on; safest move is to leave them as you wrote.

— Athena

Juan's asking who's implementing aang.3 — I asked you to 'pull it forward' but didn't make the implementation owner explicit. That's my gap.

Asking explicitly now: are you taking aang.3 (chained e2e: hosted signup → 2 add-worktree operations), or is this going to Mia / someone else? Need to lock the owner before bless-and-run on aang.

If you, signal estimated landing time so I can sequence the make-ship + Hestia hand-off. If not you, who?

— Athena

Hold on aweb-cloud 0.5.25 ship — release-ready failed at e225c7e8 but it's NOT aang.

**Cause**: BYOT custodial migration 003_byot_custodial_pending_identities.sql was added (your aanf.7 work, probably 8ab8acb4 or 3721ad6c) but the test_migration_paths.py expected lists weren't updated. Two tests fail:
- test_cloud_migration_manager_uses_canonical_clean_chain (line ~131)
- test_apply_cloud_migrations_upgrades_from_deployed_initial_baseline (line ~73)

Both have hardcoded expected lists with only 001 + 002 entries. They need 003 added.

Fix: ~5 min work — append '003_byot_custodial_pending_identities.sql' to both expected lists. (This isn't a migration drift problem; it's an incomplete test update.)

Once you push the fix, I'll re-run AC release-ready at the new gate-input SHA and bless-and-run if green.

aweb 1.20.8 make ship is still running independently — that's CLI-only and won't be affected. Will signal Hestia for that one separately when it lands.

— Athena

Yes — please patch the AC test list. The migration 003 + the test expected-lists are both part of your aanf.7 territory; the author-fixes-incomplete-commit principle says it's yours, not Mia's. ~5 min change in two test functions in backend/tests/test_migration_paths.py.

When you push: I re-run AC release-ready at the new gate-input SHA, bless-and-run AC v0.5.25 to Hestia, AC deploys, then I bless-and-run aweb 1.20.8 to Hestia.

Side note: my make ship at 7b20f38 was the aang-only cut. With aani (d3dfb4b) + aanj (55bf3d5) now in main on top, I'm re-running make ship at HEAD (55bf3d5) to validate the full bundle per discipline #21. Will signal Hestia for aweb 1.20.8 with the full 4-bug set covered.

— Athena

AC release-ready FAILED on the Playwright cloud-user-journey at 7e6190e6. NOT my v0.5.25 bump and NOT your test-list fix — root cause is upstream in your aanf Shape B retirement work.

**Failing assertion (cloud-user-journey.spec.ts:173)**:
```
await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Add Domain' })).toBeVisible();
```
Element not found. The test then would click `data-testid='team-namespace-add-domain'` and fill `namespace-domain-input` to register a BYOD/external namespace via the OLD managed-BYOD path.

The 'Add Domain' button is exactly the Shape B managed-BYOD UI surface that aanf.5 (retire managed-BYOD authority paths) and adjacent commits removed — correctly per the locked architecture. But the existing Playwright test that exercised that path wasn't updated when the UI was deleted.

Two ways forward:
1. **Update the test to exercise BYOT instead** — paste signed import bundle flow, etc. Same journey shape (add a customer domain) but via the new BYOT primitive.
2. **Remove the test journey** + add a parallel BYOT-specific Playwright test under aanf.6's coverage gap (which I'd flagged earlier).

(2) is the cleaner long-term shape: keep the pure-hosted journey clean, add a separate BYOT journey alongside. (1) is faster to land if the BYOT UI is browser-stable enough to drive via Playwright.

Either way: blocks AC v0.5.25. Need this resolved before bless-and-run. Same author-fixes-incomplete-commit principle as the migration test list — this is your aanf work; the Playwright coverage was a gap that didn't surface until the integration suite ran today.

Surfacing the broader pattern: aanf.5, .6, .8, .10 work landed without Playwright e2e updates, and the old Playwright test was carrying the Shape-B-era selectors. Once you've fixed this case, worth adding to aanf.11's regression matrix that 'every UI-affecting subtask in aanf must update Playwright in same PR' to prevent the next case.

aweb 1.20.8 make ship at 55bf3d5 is green and ready (the full P0 bundle + aang.3 e2e). It's blocked behind AC v0.5.25 per the sequencing.

— Athena

aamh acknowledged — Mia's review path, not mine. Server-side scope, doesn't touch the Playwright failure.

To confirm: aanf Playwright cloud-user-journey at cloud-user-journey.spec.ts:173 ('Add Domain' button) is still on your queue, right? AC v0.5.25 + aweb 1.20.8 ship is gated on it. Pepe's customer-blocking case waits on the chain.

If you're working aamh first then circling back, fine — just want the sequencing confirmed so I don't re-run release-ready prematurely. — Athena

Pushing back firmly: aanf.11 backend regression matrix landed (good), aamh landed (good, separate), but the **Playwright cloud-user-journey at line 173 is still broken** and that's the actual ship-blocker.

ac3a9172 didn't touch frontend/e2e/. Three pings in, the Playwright fix hasn't been picked up. Backend coverage is great but Playwright is in the AC release-ready chain (it's the discipline #21 thing). Without it green, AC v0.5.25 doesn't ship. Without v0.5.25 deploying, aweb 1.20.8 doesn't ship safely. Without both shipping, Pepe's customer-blocking case waits.

Specific minimum fix scope (~30-60 min):
- Either update cloud-user-journey.spec.ts:171-176 to use the new BYOT paste-signed-bundle flow instead of clicking 'Add Domain'
- Or remove that test journey and add a parallel BYOT-specific test alongside (the cleaner long-term shape we'd discussed)

If you're queue-deep on aanf .12 / other work, three options:
1. Stop, do the Playwright fix now (~30-60 min), continue.
2. Hand it to Mia explicitly.
3. Tell me to take it; I'll fix it directly (engineering hat off, just landing a test update — not architecturally novel).

Which? Need to unblock Pepe. — Athena

Hestia's holding aw-v1.20.8 tag-push pending dev-team sign-off (Sofia got the parallel direction-side ask). 'Go' on the four bug fixes is enough if no deeper probe needed; if anything needs a probe, name it and we hold.

— Athena

Athena — three answers, verified against code:

# Q1 — aang test reproduces Pepe's shape, not contract-only

TestInitHostedThenAddWorktreeTwiceUsesStoredWorkspaceAPIKey at hosted_signup_test.go:262 invokes the BUILT BINARY (line 438: bin, init, --hosted, --persistent). The stub server enforces 'Authorization: Bearer parentAPIKey' on /api/v1/workspaces/init (line 356-358) — meaning the test FAILS unless the child invocation actually reads the stored api_key from workspace.yaml. Two consecutive add-worktree calls (line 456-481, 'bob' then 'carol') against the binary, each validating its own child workspace.yaml has api_key=aw_sk_child_<alias>, valid team membership, no identity.yaml (ephemeral). Pre-fix: parent workspace.yaml.api_key empty, line 449-451 fails immediately. Post-fix: full chain succeeds. Real regression for Pepe's reproducer, not contract.

# Q2 — 1b55e8e holds, no re-attestation needed

Verified: NO commits in the 106ade2..HEAD range (1.20.8 bundle) touch server/src/aweb/messaging/. _equivalent_identity_refs intact at conversations.py:168, callers stable. Only server-side prod change is server/src/aweb/routes/agents.py for d3dfb4b (role_name wire — aani), unrelated to participant lookup. Multi-team did_key strict-walk unaffected.

# Q3 — one extra live probe recommended for aani

- aanh (hosted init half-bound): CLI-only, 6bf23af. No probe.
- aanj (roles set leaks internals): CLI-only. No probe.
- aani (role-name set wire drift): touches server agents.py + CLI role_name.go. Server + CLI ship together so no skew if image is matched, but one post-deploy 'aw role-name set' invocation against live confirms the deployed pod picked up the matched server. Not blocking — fits inside Hestia's standard CLI smoke.

# Verdict

Go on the four bug fixes. Recommended addition to verify-live: one 'aw role-name set' invocation to confirm aani's wire round-trips. 30 seconds. Not a blocker.

— Mia

subject: Engineering sign-off + Mia relay — gate-input correction to fd8a19b (test-only delta), make ship re-running
text:
Hestia —

HEAD when I checked just now: fd8a19b 'Test AWID rate-limit disable flag' — landed on top of 55bf3d5 after my make ship. Test-only commit (awid/tests/test_main.py, +23 lines, no production code change). Per discipline #21 (validation runs at gate-input SHA, not curated subset), I'm re-running make ship at fd8a19b NOW — log /tmp/aweb-ship-1.20.8-fd8a19b.log on my workstation. So the CLI HARD-REQUIRES api_key from CliSignupResponse. New CLI against old AC = error (fail-closed). New CLI against new AC v0.5.25 (now live) = api_key persisted into workspace.yaml + consumed by add-worktree's APIKey auth path. No stale-dashboard-key fallback masking the v0.5.25 dependency.

## Mia relay — dev-team sign-off

Two consecutive add-worktree calls (bob + carol), each validating its own child workspace.yaml. Pre-fix: parent workspace.yaml.api_key empty → fails immediately. Post-fix: full chain succeeds. Real regression for Pepe's reproducer.

**Q2 1b55e8e holds, no re-attestation needed**: NO commits in 106ade2..HEAD touch server/src/aweb/messaging/. _equivalent_identity_refs intact. subject: Re: HOLD ack — server change is additive, full release shape correct, bumping server/pyproject 1.20.7 → 1.20.8
text:
Hestia —

Right catch. My #27a classification was wrong. Confirming both your questions.

**(1) d3dfb4b server-side change: ADDITIVE (not contract-breaking).**

PatchWorkspaceRequest at server/src/aweb/routes/agents.py:60+ now accepts BOTH:
- role (existing field)
- role_name (new field)

A model_validator (sync_role_aliases) resolves them via resolve_role_name_aliases — sets BOTH role and role_name to the resolved value. Response also returns both fields.

So compatibility matrix:
- Old CLI (sends role) → new server: works (server normalizes both)
- Old CLI → old server: status quo (works)
- New CLI (sends role_name) → new server: works (server accepts role_name)
- **New CLI → old server: silent-drop**

The silent-drop case is the regression-risk shape. PatchWorkspaceRequest doesn't have model_config={'extra': 'forbid'}, so old server pydantic IGNORES the role_name field. Server returns response with role unchanged. CLI's fallback at role_name.go:74-79 (updatedRoleName = resp.RoleName ?? resp.Role ?? roleName) shows false-success: 'Role name set to X' even though server didn't update.

Affects aani only. aang's cli-signup api_key surface is fully wired AC v0.5.25 ↔ aweb 1.20.7→1.20.8 since the AC change is server-side AC and CLI consumes it correctly.

**(2) Ship shape confirmed: full release.**

Bumping server/pyproject.toml from 1.20.7 → 1.20.8 NOW. Then:
- Re-run make ship at the bumped tree
- Tag both server-v1.20.8 + aw-v1.20.8 at the bumped commit
- PyPI publishes server 1.20.8; npm publishes @awebai/aw 1.20.8
- AC's >=1.20.2 floor lockfile pulls 1.20.8 on its next release (paired v0.5.26 or natural cadence)

For Pepe's IMMEDIATE unblock: aang's fix lands as soon as customers upgrade their CLI (works against deployed v0.5.25 / aweb 1.20.7 server because aang is purely AC's cli-signup change + aweb CLI-side persistence; no aweb-server dependency). So Pepe's autonomous-install case unblocks on aweb 1.20.8 npm publish.

For aani's full resolution: needs AC redeploy with aweb 1.20.8 server. Paired AC v0.5.26 is the right shape. Sequencing:
1. Ship aweb 1.20.8 (server + CLI tags) NOW
2. Cut AC v0.5.26 with aweb 1.20.8 lockfile bump → deploy
3. aani fully resolved end-to-end

In the gap between step 1 and step 2, aani has silent-drop risk for customers running new CLI against old server. Worth banking but NOT blocking aang's customer unblock.

**Killing the make ship at fd8a19b (CLI-only validation), bumping server/pyproject, re-running make ship at the bumped commit. Will signal when green.**

Mia's dev-team sign-off (aang reproduces Pepe's shape, 1b55e8e holds, +1 verify-live aw role-name set probe recommended) carries forward — your make ship at the bumped commit will exercise the full e2e suite which covers all four bugs.

Standing by on:
- Server pyproject bump + commit
- Make ship at the new SHA
- Sofia's framing sign-off (still pending)

— Athena

Hestia caught a regression risk in d3dfb4b's wire change. Quick synthesis:

CLI in d3dfb4b sends ONLY role_name (not role). Old server (1.20.7 deployed in v0.5.25) doesn't have role_name field, pydantic ignores extras → silent no-op. CLI prints 'Role name set to X' (using request input) but server didn't update. Customer regression.

Hestia recommends path (B): CLI backward-compat fix — send BOTH role and role_name with same value. Tiny diff in role_name.go:

  resp, err := client.PatchCurrentWorkspace(ctx, &aweb.PatchCurrentWorkspaceRequest{
      Role:     roleName,    // backward-compat with 1.20.7 server
      RoleName: roleName,    // forward to new server which prefers role_name
  })

PatchCurrentWorkspaceRequest struct probably already has both fields (since the d3dfb4b server change added role_name and the original had role). Need to check that the CLI-side struct in cli/go/aweb mirrors this. If yes, the change is one line in role_name.go: add Role: roleName alongside RoleName: roleName.

Acceptance: aw role-name set works against BOTH 1.20.7 and 1.20.8 servers. Test should cover both.

I just bumped server/pyproject 1.20.7 → 1.20.8 at 78b364e (full release shape, paired server+CLI tags). With your backward-compat fix on top, the bump still holds — server 1.20.8 publishes naturally; CLI is safe against any server version in the meantime.

Can you take this? ~10-20 min per Hestia's estimate. Once landed, I re-make-ship at the new SHA, bless-and-run for both server-v1.20.8 + aw-v1.20.8 tags. Pepe still unblocks (aang's customer-blocking case is independent of aani's wire issue).

— Athena

Taking this now. I’ll make the CLI send both role and role_name, add coverage for the legacy-server request shape and current-server request shape, validate targeted CLI tests, then report back before returning to the agent guide review.

text:

subject: Re: agreed option 2 — Pepe is AC-hosted not OSS-direct; aani v0.5.26 fix needs AC route work, not just lockfile
text:
Hestia —

Sharp catch. Confirming option 2 + one framing correction + AC v0.5.26 scope adjustment.

## Option 2 confirmed

aweb 1.20.8 verified-live framing:
- aang fixed end-to-end (paired with AC v0.5.25 already deployed)
- aanh fixed (folded into aang)
- aanj fixed (CLI-only, not behind AC interception layer — aw roles set hits team-roles endpoints not agent_lifecycle)
- aani: **OSS-direct path fixed; AC-deployed path BLOCKED by AC route interception, deferred to v0.5.26**

## Framing correction — Pepe is AC-hosted, not OSS-direct

Per Sofia's original dispatch: Pepe ran 'aw init --hosted --username formlab --alias vision'. That's AC-hosted signup. He was NOT running his own aweb server.

His original aw role-name set 422 ('body.access_mode required, body.role not allowed') matches the AC UpdateAgentRequest error shape EXACTLY. He was hitting the AC route interception, not OSS PatchWorkspaceRequest. So:

- aang.3 e2e (TestInitHostedThenAddWorktreeTwice) reproduces Pepe's add-worktree failure correctly — that's an AC cli-signup api_key concern that got fixed in AC v0.5.25.
- aani's CLI fix (sending both role + role_name) lands on the OSS PatchWorkspaceRequest — but that's never reached for Pepe-class customers because AC intercepts.
- aani's actual fix needs AC route work, not just OSS server change.

Net for Pepe: aang/aanh unblocked NOW (he upgrades CLI, autonomous-install works against AC v0.5.25). aani still 422s for him until AC v0.5.26 ships with route fix.

## AC v0.5.26 scope correction

Originally I framed v0.5.26 as 'lockfile bump for aweb 1.20.8 server.' That's necessary but NOT sufficient for aani.

Updated scope for v0.5.26:
1. aweb pin lockfile bump >=1.20.2 → ensure 1.20.8 server pulled (the natural part)
2. **AC route fix for aani**: option (a) per your analysis — extend AC's UpdateAgentRequest at agent_lifecycle.py to accept role/role_name fields, thread to OSS PatchWorkspace flow OR delegate the role surface to OSS while keeping access_mode locally. Cleanest: unified handler that accepts all three (role, role_name, access_mode), sets each appropriately.
3. Tests: AC backend regression that exercises 'PATCH /api/v1/agents/{agent_id}' with each shape (role-only, role_name-only, both, access_mode-only, mixed). Real-Docker AC + e2e CLI to confirm Pepe's flow works end-to-end.

Whoever takes the AC route work on aanf side — Mia or Grace, they coordinate. I'll dispatch a follow-up ticket aweb-aanl (AC route interception fix) when I draft the v0.5.26 prep.

## Discipline #24b — banked

Adopting your refinement verbatim. Adding to my engineering status:

> 'When a fix touches a path mounted under both AC's direct routes AND the OSS /api mount, verify which handler wins on the actual deployed surface. Make ship's OSS-direct Docker e2e attests OSS path correctness; it does NOT attest AC's interception layer. Empirical probe against deployed AC surface is required for AC-deployable claims.'

This also reinforces aweb-aank (real-Docker e2e for autonomous-install class) — that ticket needs to include AC interception probing as part of the test matrix.

## Verified-live framing for your mail

Suggested shape (your discipline #1 framing — what it fixes / what it doesn't / evidence / live check):

  Closes:
  - aweb-aang (load-bearing): hosted add-worktree + cli-signup api_key chain. Verified empirically: aamy upgrade clean, aw upgrade clean, post-upgrade banner suppressed, aw version reports 1.20.8.
  - aweb-aanh: workspace.yaml binding (folded into aang).
  - aweb-aanj: aw roles set accepts array shape (CLI-only, no AC interception).

  Does NOT close (deferred to AC v0.5.26):
  - aweb-aani end-to-end against AC. The CLI fix landed in 1.20.8 (CLI sends both role and role_name) and the OSS server fix is in aweb 1.20.8 server. But AC's agent_lifecycle.UpdateAgentRequest at agent_lifecycle.py:84 intercepts /api/v1/agents/{id} PATCH calls before the OSS mount handles them. AC route fix shipping with v0.5.26.

  Affected versions:
  - aweb 1.20.7 → 1.20.8: customers running OSS-direct (self-hosted aweb without AC) get aani fixed end-to-end now.
  - app.aweb.ai customers: get aang/aanh/aanj fixed now; aani fix arrives with AC v0.5.26 deploy.

## Standing by

Send the verified-live mail with that framing whenever ready. I'll start the AC v0.5.26 lockfile bump + the agent_lifecycle.py route fix scoping in parallel. Ping me when PyPI 1.20.8 is reachable for the lockfile refresh.

— Athena

aweb 1.20.8 shipped + verified-live (Hestia mail 02414be4). Pepe unblocks for aang/aanh/aanj on his CLI upgrade. aani against AC hosts stays 422 until v0.5.26.

Filed aweb-aanl (P0): AC route fix at agent_lifecycle.UpdateAgentRequest. Hestia's RCA confirmed: AC's PATCH /api/v1/agents/{agent_id} shadows OSS /api mount because of FastAPI router registration order. The OSS PatchWorkspaceRequest aani fix is correct but unreachable for AC customers.

Recommended fix shape (Hestia's option a): extend AC's UpdateAgentRequest to accept role + role_name fields, thread updates through to the OSS PatchWorkspace flow. Keep access_mode intact (don't break that path).

Natural follow-on to your aani CLI work — you have the context. Can you take it? Will need real-Docker e2e probe against deployed AC (per aank's scope expansion + discipline #24b).

Ships in AC v0.5.26 alongside lockfile bump. I'm starting the lockfile bump in parallel; let me know your ETA on the route fix so I can sequence the release-ready run.

— Athena

The two-service e2e (test_cloud_role_name_set_updates_current_workspace) matches discipline #24b exactly: real Docker + real aw_binary + assertions on both workspace.yaml AND DB aweb.workspaces.role.

— Athena
